original_abstract: Open-access scientific resources are essential for establishing evaluation standards, providing educational platforms, and enhancing the reproducibility of experimental results. The Insight ToolKit version 4 (ITK4) builds upon this foundation by introducing modern standards for publicly available image registration methodology. Compared to its predecessors, ITK4 offers several significant architectural advances. It introduces support for multivariate images and objective functions and provides a unified framework for both high-dimensional deformation fields and low-dimensional affine transformations. This is achieved through reusable metrics and composite transforms that allow for the seamless chaining of arbitrary geometric mappings. To improve efficiency, both metrics and optimizers are designed to leverage multi-core hardware. Furthermore, ITK4 alleviates the burden of manual parameter tuning through principled heuristics that automatically scale disparate parameter types—such as rotations and translations—and constrain step sizes for gradient-based optimizers. These improvements significantly reduce the need for case-specific adjustments, allowing researchers to focus on the design and comparison of registration strategies rather than hyperparameter optimization. Ultimately, ITK4 serves as a robust infrastructure for reproducible research, offering a comprehensive foundation for evaluating new registration techniques while providing application developers with a powerful suite of tools. We demonstrate the practical utility of this framework through a reference evaluation study focused on pediatric brain labeling.
humanized_abstract: Open access research data are frequently used to define metrics for validation studies, to serve as educational examples and to facilitate the reproduction of experiments. Therefore, there is a strong need for open access research data represented in the form of high-quality algorithms using modern technology. Insight ToolKit version 4 (ITK4) provides such modern representation of widely used open access image registration techniques. ITK4 has been significantly enhanced with respect to the ITK4 ancestors. Multi-dimensional images and multi-variate objective functions are supported. High-dimensional deformation fields representing complex local transformations can be easily handled together with low-dimensional affine transformations. A large number of reusable metrics and reusable composite transforms allow to connect any type of geometric transform in a sequence. Furthermore, many metrics and all common optimizers are multi-threaded. Metrics and optimizers have heuristically-derived smart scaling formulas for vastly different parameter types such as rotations and translations, and step-size control for gradient based optimizers. As a result, the frequently needed, yet tedious and often manual, step of selecting optimal values for the parameters of the registration methods is greatly diminished, and researchers can concentrate on developing new registration algorithms and comparing them. ITK4 provides a new, robust and highly validated reference platform for reproducible research on new registration algorithms and includes all the components required for performing validation studies. We used the reference validation study on pediatric brain segmentations as an application example.
